scope: This subject deals with general methods of preparation and reactions of some organic compounds. Reactivity of organic compounds are also studied here. The syllabus emphasizes on mechanisms and orientation of reactions. Chemistry of fats and oils are also included in the syllabus.
objectives: write the structure, name and the type of isomerism of the organic compound
write the reaction, name the reaction and orientation of reactions
account for reactivity/stability of compounds
prepare organic compounds
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the structure of benzene, aromaticity and electrophilic substitution reaction of benzene and outline the structure and uses of benzene derivatives
CO2: Outline the preparation, properties of phenols and aromatic amines, Summarize the acidity of phenols and basicity of aromatic amines and explain the synthetic uses of aryl diazonium salts.
CO3: Explain the reactions of fatty acids and fats/oils and illustrate the analytical constants of fats and oils

CO4: Outline the definition and classification of polynuclear hydrocarbons and explain the preparations, reactions and uses of Naphthalene, Phenanthrene, Anthracene, Diphenylmethane, Triphenylmethane and their derivatives.
CO5: Illustrate the methods of preparations and reactions of cycloalkanes and explain the stability of Cycloalkanes

scope: Experiments involving laboratory techniques, determination of oil values, and preparation of organic compounds.
objectives: To synthesise medicinal compounds and their intermediates
To calculate percentage yield
To find out the melting point
courseOutcomes: CO1: Synthesize and recrystallize organic compounds like 2,4,6-tribromo aniline, salicylic acid, and 5-nitro salicylic acid, and find out the percentage purity.
CO2: Synthesize and recrystallize Benzanilide, 1-Phenyl azo-2-naphthol, dibenzal acetone and determine the percentage purity.
CO3: Determine the Acid value and Saponification value
CO4: Synthesize and recrystallize p-bromo acetanilide, benzil, m-dinitro benzene and determine the percentage purity.
CO5: Synthesize and recrystallize acetanilide and para bromo acetanilide

scope: The course deals with the various physical, physicochemical properties and principle involved in dosage forms, formulations. Theory and practical components of the subject help the student to get a better insight in to various areas of formulation research and development and stability studies of pharmaceuticals.
objectives: Understand various physicochemical properties of drug molecules in the designing the dosage form
Know the principles of chemical kinetics and to use them in assigning expiry date for formulation
Demonstrate use of physicochemical properties in evaluation of dosage forms
Appreciate physicochemical properties of drug molecules in formulation research and development
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain and justify the solubility of drugs, diffusion principles, phase rule and applications of distribution law
CO2: Derive and apply various theories, laws and equations related to different states of matter and to demonstrate the use of physicochemical properties of drugs in the formulation development.
CO3: Illustrate the knowledge of disperse system, its classification, theories of dispersed system, stability considerations and adsorption isotherms
CO4: Distinguish the knowledge of complexation, classification, applications in addition to protein binding of drugs
CO5: Describe the pH, Buffers and Isotonic solutions followed by the application of buffers in Pharmaceutical and biological systems

topics.topicName: Solubility expressions, mechanisms of solute solvent interactions, ideal solubility parameters, solvation and association, quantitative approach to the factors influencing solubility of drugs, diffusion principles in biological systems. Solubility of gas in liquids, solubility of liquids in liquids, (Binary solutions, ideal solutions) Raoult‘s law, real solutions. partially miscible liquids, Critical solution temperature and applications. Distribution law, its limitations and applications.
topics.name: Solubility expressions, mechanisms of solute solvent interactions, ideal solubility parameters, solvation and association, quantitative approach to the factors influencing solubility of drugs, diffusion principles in biological systems. Solubility of gas in liquids, solubility of liquids in liquids, (Binary solutions, ideal solutions) Raoult‘s law, real solutions. partially miscible liquids, Critical solution temperature and applications. Distribution law, its limitations and applications.

objectives: Understand various physiochemical properties of drug molecules in designing the dosage form
Know the principles of Chemical kinetics and to use them in assigning expiry date for formulation
Demonstrate the use of physiochemical properties in evaluation of dosage form
Appreciate physiochemical properties of drug molecules in Formulation, Research and Development
courseOutcomes: CO1: Apply the knowledge of phase diagram to determine consolute temperatures
CO2: Analyse the concept of solubility and recognize basic rules and equations regarding physical principles
CO3: Create the knowledge of CMC and to determine HLB
CO4: Evaluate the phenomenon of partition coefficient and surface tension
CO5: Apply the phenomenon of surface tension

scope: In the broadest sense, scope of microbiology is the study of all organisms that are invisible to the naked eye- that is the study of microorganisms. Microorganisms are necessary for the production of bread, cheese, beer, antibiotics, vaccines, vitamins, enzymes etc. Microbiology has an impact on medicine, agriculture, food science, ecology, genetics, biochemistry, immunology etc.
objectives: Understand methods of identification, cultivation and preservation of various microorganisms
Importance of sterilization in microbiology and pharmaceutical industry
Learn sterility testing of pharmaceutical products
Microbiological standardization of Pharmaceuticals
Understand the cell culture technology and its applications in pharmaceutical industries
courseOutcomes: CO1: Describe the morphology, isolation, nutritional requirements and preservation of the microorganism in addition to the cultivation and measurement of microbial growths.

CO2: Evaluate the various sterilization techniques used in the pharmaceutical and microbiology laboratory followed by the analysis of the efficiency of various sterilization methods using sterility indicators.
CO3: Explain the different types of disinfectants and antiseptics with its mechanisms followed by the microbial validation of the pharmaceutical products.
CO4: Evaluate the microbiological assay of pharmaceutical substances, newer antibiotics and comprehending the aseptic areas and equipment’s used in the pharmaceutical industry.

CO5: Summarise the causes of various microbial contaminants of pharmaceutical products in the industrial areas and further to illustrate the animal cell culture technique in pharmaceutical industrial research.

scope: Practical exercises in equipments, sterilization, subculturing, staining, pure culture isolation, microbiological assay, motility, sterility, and water analysis.
objectives: Explore the equipment used in microbiology laboratory and sterilization methods
Know the morphological identification methods
Carry out the isolation of pure culture by using different methods
Explore the microbial sensitivity of the antibiotics against the microbes
Know the identification of microbes using biochemical tests
courseOutcomes: CO1: Demonstrate the various equipments, processing using in the microbiology laboratory, sterilization process and to sterilize the prepared media
CO2: Determine the sub culturing methods of growing the bacteria and fungi for nutrient slants preparations and identifying the morphology of the microbes with their categories.
CO3: Determine the isolation procedure to get the pure culture by using different methods
CO4: Estimation of antibiotics effect by utilizes various prescribed methods.
CO5: Analyze the water quality and test the sterility of the pharmaceutical preparations and biochemical tests to identify the specific bacteria.

scope: This course is designed to impart a fundamental knowledge on the art and science of various unit operations used in pharmaceutical industry.
objectives: To know various unit operations used in Pharmaceutical industries
To understand the material handling techniques
To perform various processes involved in pharmaceutical manufacturing process
To carry out various test to prevent environmental pollution
To appreciate and comprehend significance of plant layout design for optimum use of resources
To appreciate the various preventive methods used for corrosion control in Pharmaceutical industries
courseOutcomes: CO1: Illustrate the basic concepts of fluid flow, flow meters and explaining about the concepts, laws, working and construction of mills involving size reduction and size separation.
CO2: Describe the equipment’s related to evaporation, distillation, heat interchangers with its mechanism and applications.

CO3: Explain the equilibrium moisture content, drying rate from drying curves, theory of mixing in addition to working procedure, art and theory of mixing, solidsolid, solid-liquid and liquid-liquid mixing equipment.

CO4: Summarise the principle and theory of filtrations and centrifugation in addition to filter aids, filter media, industrial filters, laboratory filtration equipments, centrifugal filters and centrifugal sedimentors.
CO5: Evaluate the significance of plant layout design for optimum use of resources in pharmaceutical industries followed by the types and various preventive methods used for corrosion control.

referenceBooks.author: Lachmann
referenceBooks.title: Theory and practice of industrial pharmacy

scope: Experiments in heat transfer, filtration, drying curves, crystallization, humidity, tablet machinery, size analysis, size reduction and mixing.
objectives: To evaluate the equipment related to heat transfer, milling, filtration, drying, crystallization, evaporation, extraction and its working
To determine wet bulb and dry bulb temperatures and to plot the humidity charts. To evaluate the factors affecting the rate of filtration and to calculate the radiation constant
To determine the rate of evaporation and crystallization and to find the factors affecting the centrifugation
To evaluate the moisture content, drying rate from the drying curves
To evaluate the size separation by sieving method and determination of particle size done by size reduction by ball mill using Rittinger’s law
courseOutcomes: CO1: Finding the equipment related to heat transfer, milling, filtration, drying, crystallization, evaporation and extraction and its working.
CO2: Determining the wet bulb and dry bulb temperatures and to plot the humidity charts and the factors affecting the rate of filtration and to calculate the radiation constant.
CO3: Determining the rate of evaporation, crystallization and the factors affecting the centrifugation.
CO4: Evaluating the moisture content, drying rate from the drying curves.
CO5: Determining the size separation by sieving method and particle size done by size reduction using Rittinger’s law.
